description: Universal operational_notes that apply to ALL platforms. Merged into every platform's knowledge['global']['operational_notes'] at load time by spark.tasks.knowledge_loader.load_knowledge. PLATFORM-AGNOSTIC ONLY; recipe-grade detail lives in each platform's screen_types/*.yaml. Rendered size budget: <=5K.

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY ARTICLE screen on ANY platform
operational_notes.rule: ARTICLE = SCROLL-TO-BOTTOM + EXTRACT, THEN ADVANCE. Every article scrolls gradually to the bottom (completion is often scroll-tracked; the advance affordance lives at the bottom) AND carries an extract config so the content lands in the user's local KB before advancing. An article map without scroll+extract is wrong by definition. Recipe detail: the platform's ARTICLE yaml.
operational_notes.scope: UNIVERSAL — every ARTICLE screen variant on every platform.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Advance-only BT (no scroll, no extract). Closing the article via X/back (bypasses progress tracking).
operational_notes.example_khan: 'Learn and try' interactive articles: scroll-through marks completion; embedded Check blocks are optional; advance is the exact 'Up next: <x>' AXLink at the bottom.

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY screen with apostrophe/quote/typographic Unicode in an element name
operational_notes.rule: SMART-QUOTE EXACT MATCH. Click targets must COPY THE EXACT Unicode characters from the captured tree — platforms render curly U+2019/U+2018/U+201C/U+201D, not ASCII ' and ". Exact matching fails on the wrong variant; never retype names, never auto-correct typography. If a verbatim-copied name still returns NONE, fall back to click_at on the element's visible_bbox center (points, same coord space).
operational_notes.example_khan: 'Let’s go' uses U+2019; find_and_click with ASCII apostrophe returned NONE (verified 2026-05-19).

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY EXERCISE screen whose question references images, diagrams, charts, or visual states
operational_notes.rule: IMAGE-DEPENDENT QUESTIONS REQUIRE FULL IMAGE DESCRIPTION IN CONTEXT, produced in the DISCOVER/extraction phase BEFORE the solve call. If the question references visual content (AXImage in the question area, or wording like image/figure/diagram/before-and-after/graph/each-system), per-image descriptions are as important as option extraction: use the vision-grounded question type, instruct explicit per-image description before answering, analyze multiple referenced sub-systems INDEPENDENTLY, and capture additional frames (scroll + re-screenshot) when the figure exceeds the viewport — pass ALL frames. Recipe detail: each platform's exercise yamls.
operational_notes.scope: UNIVERSAL — every platform, every exercise subtype that uses images.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Text-only solve on an image-grounded question; assuming the LLM infers image-dependence without explicit instruction; single screenshot of a clipped figure.

operational_notes.screen_variant: ANY wrong-answer outcome on ANY exercise on ANY platform
operational_notes.rule: WRONG ANSWER = CATASTROPHIC FAILURE = FULL STOP. ONE canonical policy (R3): one worker shot -> verdict detected (Not quite / Incorrect / red feedback — regardless of tree movement) -> FULL STOP -> escalation ladder with full prior-attempt context (RCA at tier 1: was KB retrieval relevant? were images described? was the question type vision-grounded? were all options/menus enumerated verbatim?) -> definitions improved (yaml/quirk edits) -> terminal user-assist WITH the correct answer. NO RETRIES on a wrong answer — not with the same context, not with 'improved' context; the corrected attempt happens only as the ladder's outcome, never as a reflex.
operational_notes.scope: UNIVERSAL — every wrong-answer state on every platform. Wrong-answer DETECTION mechanics are per-platform (verdict text, feedback styling) and live in that platform's yamls.
operational_notes.rationale: Jesse 2026-05-20: with appropriate context there is nothing on these platforms the solver cannot get right every time; every wrong answer is a system failure requiring root-cause analysis. Jesse 2026-06-12 (R3): no retries on wrong answer; full investigation.
operational_notes.anti_pattern: Try-again reflex resubmission; treating wrong answers as routine; escalating without the prior answer + verdict signal in the packet.
